title: Rich Tender Japanese Wagyu Gyudon
description: A romantic Japanese dish featuring tender Wagyu beef, served over a bed of rice in a savory sauce, with a nutty flavor profile.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and blanch the Wagyu beef slices for 30 seconds to remove excess blood.
Remove the beef from the water with a slotted spoon and set it aside on a plate.
In a large saucepan, combine the soy sauce, sake, mirin, and sugar, and bring to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the sauce for 5 minutes, or until it has thickened slightly.
Add the sesame oil, green onions, and sesame seeds to the sauce and stir to combine.
Add the blanched Wagyu beef slices to the sauce and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until they are coated in the sauce.
Season the beef with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the Wagyu beef over a bed of Japanese rice and garnish with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
Cook the Japanese rice according to the package instructions and set it aside.
To assemble the dish, place a scoop of cooked rice onto a plate or bowl, then add a few slices of the Wagyu beef on top.
